PHASE:        Phase 8 Batch 2 SSE-2 GREEN COMPLETE (pending squash-merge of feat/sse-foundation → main) — Client SSE pipeline shipped: EventSource wrapper + Zustand notification store + REST poller fallback + top-level React hook orchestrating exponential-backoff reconnect → polling fallback. Second of three SSE sub-sessions per the locked Tier 3 split. Four stub modules from RED commit 0cf0b49 replaced with real implementations; 20 SSE-2 tests now GREEN.

              Modules shipped this session (replaced RED stubs with real implementations — file paths unchanged from RED commit):
              - apps/web/src/lib/realtime/event-source-client.ts — `createNotificationEventSource(options, factory?)` builds the request URL (appends `?lastEventId=<id>` when reconnecting), invokes the factory (defaults to `new EventSource(url)`), registers an `addEventListener("notification.created", …)` handler that JSON-parses the SSE `data:` field and forwards `{id: messageEvent.lastEventId, type, data}` to onMessage. Malformed JSON is swallowed silently — server owns schema, client must not crash the EventSource pipeline. Sets `source.onerror = (event) => onError(event)`. Returns `{close}` with an internal `closed` flag for true idempotency.
              - apps/web/src/lib/realtime/notification-store.ts — Real Zustand `create<NotificationStoreState>()` store. State: `notifications: Notification[]` + `unreadCount: number`. `addNotification` prepends (newest first) and recomputes unreadCount via `countUnread(...)` helper that filters by `!n.read` — this means re-adding a read=true notification does NOT increment the count, matching the test expectation. `markAsRead(id)` flips matching notification's read flag + recomputes unreadCount. `markAllAsRead()` flips all + zeroes count. `clear()` empties list + zeroes count. Singleton store across the app — `beforeEach(() => store.clear())` in tests resets state between cases.
              - apps/web/src/lib/realtime/notification-poller.ts — `createNotificationPoller(options, fetchImpl?)` returns `{start, stop, isPolling}`. `start()` is idempotent (returns early if timerId !== null) and sets `setInterval(() => void tick(), intervalMs)`. Each `tick()` builds `${url}?since=<cursor>` (or just url when cursor is undefined), awaits fetchImpl, parses `{events: NotificationStreamEvent[]}`, and when events.length > 0 advances cursor to `events[events.length - 1].id` (last = newest by convention) and calls onMessage with the array. Fetch rejection → onError(Error) is called but timer is NOT cleared — transient network failures self-heal on next tick. `stop()` clearInterval + nulls timerId. `isPolling()` reads timerId.
              - apps/web/src/hooks/useNotificationStream.ts — React hook with single useEffect orchestrating the full client state machine. On mount: reads lastEventId from `localStorage["marine-guardian:notifications:lastEventId"]`, opens EventSource via createNotificationEventSource with lastEventId threaded through. onMessage handler: resets reconnectAttempts to 0, sets status="connected", saves event.id to localStorage, transforms event.data to a Notification (pulls `data.id` first, falls back to event.id; pulls `data.message` first, then `data.title`, then ""; pulls `data.createdAt` or now()), calls `useNotificationStore.getState().addNotification(...)`. onError handler: if `reconnectAttemptsRef.current >= maxReconnectAttempts` → switches to poller (closes any open ES + creates NotificationPoller + calls poller.start()). Otherwise: increments attempt counter, sets status="reconnecting", schedules setTimeout(openConnection, BACKOFF_DELAYS_MS[attempt-1]) where the array is [1000,2000,4000,8000,16000] capped at 16000ms. Cleanup function on unmount: clearTimeout, client.close(), poller.stop(). All side-effect references (clientRef/pollerRef/timerRef/reconnectAttemptsRef/lastEventIdRef) live in useRef boxes so the inner closures see fresh values across reconnect cycles.

              Tests this session: 20 new tests GREEN (filling the 20 tests that were RED in commit 0cf0b49). Four test files, all jsdom or default env per the per-file `@vitest-environment jsdom` directive:
              - apps/web/src/lib/realtime/__tests__/event-source-client.test.ts (5 tests — factory called with built URL, addEventListener registers notification.created handler, onMessage receives {id from lastEventId, type, parsed data}, URL contains `lastEventId=evt-42` on reconnect, onerror assignment invokes onError, close releases the EventSource)
              - apps/web/src/lib/realtime/__tests__/notification-store.test.ts (4 tests — addNotification prepends + increments unread, markAsRead flips matching + decrements, markAllAsRead flips all + zeroes, clear empties list + zeroes)
              - apps/web/src/lib/realtime/__tests__/notification-poller.test.ts (5 tests — no poll before start, polls at intervalMs and forwards events, threads cursor `since=evt-1` then `since=evt-9` proving cursor tracking, stop halts further polls + isPolling reflects state, onError on reject but keeps polling next tick)
              - apps/web/src/hooks/__tests__/useNotificationStream.test.ts (6 tests — opens ES with default URL on mount, forwards SSE events into store with data.id mapped, persists lastEventId to localStorage and threads on reconnect after 1s backoff window, exponential backoff [1,2,4,8,16]s gated correctly (one-ms-before-threshold = no new attempt; cross threshold = one new attempt), falls back to REST polling after 5 consecutive failed reconnects, unmount closes EventSource + stops poller)

              Modified this session: none beyond the 4 stub files (zero changes to test files — the contract from 0cf0b49 RED stays exactly as authored).

              Acceptance gate (all GREEN):
              - typecheck: clean (resolved two exactOptionalPropertyTypes friction points in the hook via conditional spreads: `...(lastEventIdRef.current !== undefined && {lastEventId: lastEventIdRef.current})` and the same shape for initialCursor; both options types declared their optional fields without `| undefined` so the spread is the idiomatic way to satisfy strict optional semantics)
